title: Pungent Fermented Russian Dish Pilaf
description: A spicy, crunchy, fermented Russian dish made with buckwheat groats, featuring chilli flakes and a dairy-free friendly sauce. This dish is perfect for those who enjoy a monsoon of flavors.
instructions: In a mortar and pestle, pound the ginger and galangal until they are well crushed.
Add the chilli flakes, onion, and garlic to the mortar and pestle and pound until they are well combined.
In a large saucepan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the buckwheat groats to the saucepan and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until they are lightly toasted.
Add the apple cider vinegar, water, and sugar to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the buckwheat is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Transfer the buckwheat mixture to the mortar and pestle and pound until it is well combined with the spice mixture.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and let it cool to room temperature before serving.
